title: PaymentAction::Address handler: wire next_diversifier to response
description: In cli/src/commands.rs (or wherever PaymentAction::Address is handled), replace any stub or cached-address return with a call to wallet.next_diversifier() then diversified_address(index). This is the final wiring step for nie-bgx. Defensive programming: if wallet is unavailable (wallet not initialized), return a user-visible error message in chat, not a panic or silent no-op; log only the diversifier index on success, never the address bytes (addresses are public but logging them links payments — log 'issued fresh subaddress at index N'); validate that the returned UA is non-empty before sending. Use a subagent to trace the existing PaymentAction dispatch path from slash command handler to confirm the insertion point.
acceptance_criteria: Integration smoke test: call /receive twice in a session, assert the two addresses differ. Test: with no wallet initialized, /receive prints a helpful error message.
close_reason: PaymentAction::Address stub replaced with real diversified_address() call. WalletFvks loaded at chat startup from wallet.key (passphrase prompt or no_passphrase). No wallet → Unknown reply + helpful message. Consecutive requests produce distinct UAs. Tests: with-wallet, no-wallet, consecutive-distinct.
